reviews.review_title: Should be a mandatory experience for all
reviews.review_description: My first VR experience, and I feel should be mandatory for everyone! It's just a wonderful way to be introduced to VR. You could, honestly, sit there and play around with the three environments they provide for hours. A lot more fun than it has a right to be! NOTE: Not sure what the difference between the original First Steps and this one is(went through both just to see), but still excellent stuff.
I remember booting up this game when I first got the quest 2 it was the most funniest times ever of my life I will forever like this game
I remember the first times launching this.. it was the best experience of my life!
It can teach you how to use your controls and know what to do my sis is 6 and she’s doing perfect
ITS VERY GOOD just wow im blown away by how good it is i cant describe how good it is

It's just me, or does this game crash my VR
The levels can't be completed 😭😔;
The instructions are unclear and worst of all: the game hijacks my VR and won't let me play other games, even after restarting the headset!!!

NOW FOR THE POSITIVES😃:
It's fun, the quality is good 👍😊, and luckily the VR issues are still reverseible. I really enjoyed 😀 playing with the robot 🤖 and those little toys 🪀, and flying the plane was great 👍 too - everything is well - made and runs long enough to be engaging.

Overetall, thanks for the experience, but with these major issues, it's still only 2 stars from me.

Well, every time I keep playing, it keeps glitching me and taking me to this game

I work at a hospital and provide VR for patients to pass the time and give them fun activities to do. I always use this app first because not only does it help get you understanding the controllers- the mini games makes it not feel like a tutorial.

It's one of the best apps ever in VR World.
But I am eagerly waiting for the update and new features to be available in the "first step".

Very user friendly app, built with emotions, creativity, detailed features and much more.

reviews.review_title: Nice experience for first-time VR users.
reviews.review_description: Nice experience for first-time VR users.
This is the perfect way a beginner should start on vr and I recommend it for anyone to try it! Love it!!

This was a great demo - it really helped me learn how to use the controller well with gripping, picking stuff up, and how to use the buttons.   
My favorite part is dancing with the robot.  I wish their was just an app like that.  I liked the robot mimicking my moves and spinning the robot around.  I would love if there was a whole game just to dance with the robot instead of the dance games where you have to mimic the instructors.  I'd rather dance in VR just for fun and freestyle with the cute robot.
It’s a great learning tool for those who can stand, but for a person that must play seated, it doesn’t work as well, starting with table being above your head.

Honestly, I would not complain if this became something more. I've visited this "intro" many times, as have my kids. We all enjoy a visit and have a blast with the mini-games. Please give this, and other passive games, more attention!

reviews.review_title: I cried so hard dancing with the robot
reviews.review_description: The first app I experienced when I put on my Quest2. I didn’t expect much as I tried VR like 3 years ago and I didn’t get much fun. However this time I got so amazed by the grabbing features — I didn’t know I can use my fingers in VR now! And when I see the cute robot and shake hands with him, it suddenly reminds me once I gave a speech on my grandpa who passed away many years ago. He is still living in my heart but there is no way to prove it, and I can only imagine me shaking hands with him in my mind. However when I’m shaking hands with the robot and dancing with him, I suddenly realize the world has changed and we can now see and play with the ghosts! I keep repeating dancing with the robot and my tears keep dropping to the ground. It’s impossible to share the experience but it’s so invaluable to me…. Quest2 will change the world, for sure! My sincere thanks to the creators of the game, you guys are marvelous!

reviews.review_title: Best onboarding app for VR ever!
reviews.review_description: Not just a beginner-friendly tutorial, it's also a really good experience. Made me tear up at one point!

Easy to understand app that teaches you how to move in the vr world using your controllers and/or virtual hands. When showing people how awesome my occulus is I have them go into this app before exploring or playing anything else. That way they know how to use their virtual hands and don't break my controllers.

I really love this experience, i will never forget the moment that i booted my oculus for the first time and played this game. It was so wholesome, the other game with the cute little robot is also great to play after this one.
Nothing but a waste of space
I just got my Oculus Quest 2 and I'll said that this introduction just blew my mind. Is amazing the way this app show you how to interact with different objects. Pretty good app!!!
Great introduction to the VR world for beginners. I’m not new to VR, but I believe this is a must have for those family and friends interested in VR.

New to vr just got my headset today and I’m amazed by the graphics and how you can interact with objects it’s truly amazing how far technology has come. I’ll be sure to check out other games with this beauty! Definitely worth the install!
Beautiful way to introduce people to VR. I don’t know why I’m telling you this, you’ve already seen it.
Best introduction to vr

I know this was meant to be a demo/tutorial on the controls, but I got so caught up in throwing the paper airplane,  dancing with the robot and shooting off the rockets that I stayed in there far longer than I needed to.  What do you have to lose?  It is cute, fun and teaches you control basics.  Also...DANCING WITH A ROBOT!!!
It is great (made me smile to dance with a cute robot) and very well made for anyone as a first VR experience!

When it starts you are under the floor and there does not appear to be any way to adjust the height.  Strange that all other games work just fine..  Since this is free, you can try it but for me, its a complete waste.

Its an introduction minigame with simple rules and basic play. I love the fact that theres no stakes in play, so i find myself coming back to it over and over again just to dance with my robot friend (who ive named barron) or to aim tiny bottle rockets at a blimp. (I havnt hit it yet) there isnt much to it, but i like that about it. A simple into game with just enough charm to bring you back.

As a new user of hand control VR I found this to be very helpful in practicing how to use the buttons and trigger. So I have given it 5 * because it is a good way to get to know what the Quest 2 can do.
Absolutely loved it, great introduction to VR and the dancing robot is amazing!
I have tried psvr before and this was so fresh and new. The perfect app to load first on your new quest 2 👏👏👏

And now even better on Quest2!
Must have!
Great sample what VR can be.

Quest 2 has no menu home library. So disappointed.  Must return.  I tried reset 3 times. And no home library. I cant do anything.  But look at the controllers on the vr. I tried the oculus button and only the grid shows up but no home menu library.
This game is so fun and the blimp is so fun to control everything is great have a good time
Adorable little robot buddy my goodness I love him. Also very cool graphics in the beginning.

reviews.review_title: The best free app for a VR Headset
reviews.review_description: I know that this game is a tutorial but I find myself coming back to it very often. It's not because I don't know how to use the controllers, but because this game is gorgeous and fun! The game looks and sounds beautiful and has realistic physics. The toys that you can play with are extremely entertaining and they never get boring. The tutorial aspect of the game is also very helpful. Personally, I prefer the dancing robot game to the shooter game, but that's just because I don't play many shooters. Overall, I would highly recommend this game to people who are fairly young and to people who have never used an oculus before. :)
